name: White Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Butter a 9-inch springform pan. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
To make the crust: Combine flour, sugar, melted butter, and graham cracker crumbs. Press firmly into the bottom of the prepared springform pan. Bake at 325°F for approximately 15 minutes. Cool.
To make the filling:
Melt 1 lb. white chocolate and heavy cream in a double boiler until smooth. Remove from heat; cool.
In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese with an electric mixer until smooth. Add sour cream; blend until smooth. Add egg yolks; blend well. Add vanilla; blend. Add white chocolate mixture; blend well.
In another bowl, whip egg whites, sugar, and cream of tartar with an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Fold 1/3 of the egg white mixture into the batter. Fold the remaining egg white mixture into the batter. Add 1/4 lb. chopped white chocolate.
Place a 2-quart pan half-filled with water beneath the cheesecake in the oven; bake at 325°F for 1 hour. Turn off the oven; leave the cheesecake in the oven with the door closed for 1 hour. Remove from the oven; cool. Refrigerate 4 hours or until set.
Topping: Melt 8 oz. white chocolate; spread over the top of the cheesecake. Garnish with strawberries.
